fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to new mexico, speech-language pathologist compact new mexico, new mexico speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology, Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensing Practices Board
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: Full credential for independent SLP practice in New Mexico.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: You must complete a master’s degree or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation (CAA) in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience that includes at least 1260 hours, with at least 36 weeks of full-time experience.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by the Educational Testing Service (ETS).

telepractice_text: New Mexico permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. New Mexico is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: New Mexico issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: New Mexico does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico SLP license cost?
Is New Mexico part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does New Mexico allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, with a valid NM license.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: New Mexico does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in New Mexico
hero.dek: New Mexico Speech-Language Pathology licenses require a master’s degree, passing the Praxis exam, and completion of a supervised professional experience.
